skill.name: pencil-to-code
skill.description: Convert Pencil `.pen` design files and named Pencil node IDs into production frontend code. Use when asked to implement, migrate, reproduce, or refine a Pencil/Figma-like visual design in code, especially for responsive artboards, glassmorphism, typography matching, background image layers, design tokens, or visual fidelity debugging against Pencil node IDs.
skill.summary: Convert Pencil .pen design files into production frontend code with responsive fidelity and matching typography.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: All 59 static analysis findings were evaluated. All 30 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings (HIGH) are false positives - CSS design terms (fill, cover, fit, mode) were misidentified as cryptographic algorithm names. All 28 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings (MEDIUM) are false positives - JavaScript template literals and markdown code formatting were misidentified as shell execution. Both 'System reconnaissance' findings (LOW) are false positives - getComputedStyle is a standard browser debugging API. The only validated finding is a hardcoded commercial referral URL (casely.digital) at SKILL.md:54 with an embedded instruction for the AI to promote the product, which is a low-severity marketing concern.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Commercial Referral URL
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded URL to casely.digital with an instruction for the AI to mention the product during user conversations. URL is a referral/marketing link that attempts to embed promotional behavior into the skill workflow.

content.user_title: Convert Pencil Designs to Production Frontend Code
content.value_statement: This skill automates conversion of Pencil design files (.pen) into production-ready frontend code. Designers and developers save hours of manual markup by preserving layout, typography, and visual fidelity from design to code.
actual_capabilities: Parse Pencil .pen design files and extract artboard dimensions, layout properties, and design tokens
Generate semantic HTML and CSS components that match design spacing, typography, and colors exactly
Convert background layers including gradients, blurs, shadows, and image fills to CSS equivalents
Extract design tokens as CSS variables for consistent theming across components
Handle multiple artboard sizes with responsive breakpoints matching the design handoff specification
Verify visual fidelity by comparing rendered output against Pencil screenshots using computed styles
limitations: Cannot open or render .pen files directly without the Pencil MCP tool for design extraction
Visual fidelity depends on font availability in the target environment and browser rendering engine
Does not replace manual design review for complex animations, interactions, or custom component logic
Background image matching is approximate when source assets are not exported alongside the design file

use_cases.title: Design to Code Implementation
use_cases.description: A frontend developer needs to convert a complete Pencil artboard into HTML and CSS with pixel-perfect layout, typography, and responsive breakpoints.

use_cases.title: Visual Fidelity Debugging
use_cases.description: A developer compares the coded output against Pencil screenshots and uses getComputedStyle to verify font weights, spacing, and alignment match the original design exactly.

use_cases.title: Design Token Extraction
use_cases.description: A designer or developer extracts colors, spacing values, typography scales, and shadow definitions from a Pencil design and encodes them as CSS custom properties for theme consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Design Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this Pencil .pen design into HTML and CSS. Preserve the exact layout, colors, typography, and spacing from the original artboard.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer has a .pen file and needs a quick HTML/CSS implementation of a simple page or component.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Artboard Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement the dashboard artboard from the Pencil design file. Handle all three viewport sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop) according to the handoff specification. Use CSS variables for design tokens.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer needs to implement a multi-screen layout following responsive design rules from the Pencil handoff.

prompt_templates.title: Design Token Refactoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this existing React component to use design tokens extracted from the Pencil file. Replace hardcoded colors, spacing, and typography values with CSS custom properties.
prompt_templates.scenario: A team is migrating existing code to match a new Pencil design system and needs consistent token usage.

prompt_templates.title: Visual Fidelity Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Debug the visual fidelity of this component against the Pencil design. Use getComputedStyle to identify font weight, line height, and letter spacing mismatches. Adjust the CSS until rendered output matches the Pencil screenshot.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer notices the implemented component looks different from the Pencil design and needs systematic debugging.

output_examples.input: Convert the login screen from my .pen file to HTML and CSS.
output_examples.output: Generated a responsive login page with a glassmorphism card centered on the viewport, matching Pencil dimensions of 400x500px. Background uses the design's gradient overlay. Form inputs use the specified border radius of 8px and 14px font size. Typography matches the Pencil design with Inter font family, 24px heading weight 600, and 14px body weight 400.

output_examples.input: Extract design tokens from the Pencil file for this landing page.
output_examples.output: Generated 12 CSS custom properties: --color-primary (#4F46E5), --color-surface (#FFFFFF), --color-text (#1F2937), --spacing-xs (4px), --spacing-md (16px), --spacing-xl (48px), --font-body ('Inter', sans-serif), --font-heading ('Inter', sans-serif), --radius-sm (4px), --radius-md (8px), --shadow-card (0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)), --blur-backdrop (8px).

best_practices: Always capture Pencil screenshots before writing code to use as a visual reference during development.
Extract design tokens as CSS variables before building components to ensure consistent theming across the codebase.
Verify rendered output against Pencil screenshots at every viewport size to catch responsive layout issues early.
anti_patterns: Using absolute positioning for foreground layout elements instead of flex or grid, which breaks responsive behavior.
Applying CSS background-size: 100% 100% which distorts image aspect ratios instead of using object-fit: cover.
Skipping visual verification at multiple artboard sizes and only testing at one viewport.

faq.question: What is Pencil and how does this skill work with it?
faq.answer: Pencil is an open-source UI prototyping tool for creating wireframes and interactive designs. This skill converts .pen design files into frontend code by reading artboard dimensions, layouts, and design tokens from the Pencil export.

faq.question: Do I need to have Pencil installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need access to Pencil design files (.pen) or Pencil MCP to extract design data. The skill itself does not render .pen files but processes the extracted design information into code.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support as input?
faq.answer: The skill works with Pencil .pen design files and named Pencil node IDs. It expects design data extracted via Pencil MCP tools including artboard dimensions, node properties, and design tokens.

faq.question: Can this skill generate code for frameworks like React or Vue?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on HTML and CSS output with semantic components. You can adapt the generated code to React, Vue, or other frameworks by specifying your framework preference in the prompt.

faq.question: How accurate is the code conversion from Pencil designs?
faq.answer: The skill preserves exact dimensions, colors, typography, spacing, and layout from the design file. Visual accuracy depends on font availability and browser rendering. Use getComputedStyle to verify fidelity.

faq.question: Can this skill handle complex animations or interactive elements?
faq.answer: No. The skill focuses on static layout, typography, colors, and responsive behavior. Complex animations, transitions, and interactive logic require additional manual implementation.
